_meta.description: Deactivate a user in Okta. Navigates to the user's profile page via /admin/users search, then clicks Deactivate. DESTRUCTIVE — confirm before running.

auth.note: Okta OAuth PKCE — session must already be active.

user_identifier.description: Name or email of the user to deactivate. Used to find the user in the table.
user_identifier.hint: Can be first name, last name, or email. Example: 'Hermes AI' or 'hermes@hermes.ai'

_meta.note_params: IMPORTANT: {{base_url}} in chain steps is resolved from API-call params, NOT from payload.params. When calling via /run directly, pass params={"base_url": "https://trial-8689388-admin.okta.com"} explicitly, OR use the hardcoded URL (chains already use hardcoded URL as of this version).

user-search-box.description: Search textarea on the users list page
user-search-box.resolveHint: Textarea with placeholder 'Search for users by first name, primary email or username'

deactivate-button.description: Deactivate button on user profile page — inside More Actions dropdown or inline actions
deactivate-button.resolveHint: Look for a 'Deactivate' link or button. May be inside a 'More Actions' dropdown menu on the user profile page. Also check [data-se='lifecycle-actions'] area.
deactivate-button.note: DEPRECATED: data-se selector does not exist in OIE. Use click_text flow instead: More Actions → Deactivate → OK. See payload 08.

deactivate-confirm-button.description: Confirm deactivation in the confirmation dialog
deactivate-confirm-button.resolveHint: Confirmation dialog button after clicking Deactivate. Look for 'Deactivate' or 'Confirm' button inside a modal.

chain.note: Navigate to Okta admin users page

Find the user row in the table by name or email. Okta table has columns: Person & username, Primary email, Status. NOTE: Okta table rows contain name+username concatenated (e.g., 'Hermes AIhermes@hermes.ai'). The identifier should match on first_name or email. find_row uses substring matching.
Click on the user's name link to navigate to their profile page
Wait for user profile page to load
Read the user profile page to find deactivate controls. Look for Deactivate button or More Actions dropdown.
Click the Deactivate button. If not visible, may need to open More Actions dropdown first.
Wait for confirmation dialog to appear
Read the confirmation dialog to understand what's being confirmed
Confirm deactivation — user status should now show Deactivated/Inactive
